title: OptionIter PAD1 handling uses unbounded recursion
description:
file: rust/lichen-rpl/src/message.rs
line: 406-409

The OptionIter::next() method handles PAD1 bytes (type 0x00) via recursive self.next() call:

```rust
if opt_type == OPT_PAD1 {
    self.pos += 1;
    return self.next();  // Recursive
}
```

A malicious packet with many consecutive PAD1 bytes could overflow the call stack. On constrained embedded devices with 4KB stack, ~60-100 PAD1 bytes could be enough to trigger overflow.

Fix: Convert to iterative loop that skips PAD1 bytes without recursion.
